110014.explanation: The board should be a reflection of society.

110015.explanation: The municipal council is elected by the residents and is where decision-making takes place. Residents’ opinions are collected through participation, but a referendum goes too far.

110018.explanation: Farmers should be facilitated in other income models, but not every place in our beautiful landscape is suitable for a campsite. It must be tailored to the situation.

110019.explanation: It is good to provide support, but money is not always the best way. It can also be about administrative support, for example. But that also costs the municipality money, so that is why we answered “agree.”

110021.explanation: We want to comply with the distribution law and regionally we agreed to realize 80 additional places for unaccompanied minor asylum seekers (AMVs). We adhere to that. Such a location is not an asylum seekers’ center (AZC), which is why we answered “disagree.” But we do want an AMV location.

110022.explanation: This can be undesirable if it ruins our special landscape.

110023.explanation: We do that ourselves, but it is too much to ask this from private individuals.

110030.explanation: We give residents on social assistance a freely disposable amount. They may use it for a sports club, but they do not have to.

110035.explanation: Preservation of monuments is important; we do not have that many.

110036.explanation: There is no reason for that.

110037.explanation: There is no reason for that.

110038.explanation: We make regional agreements about necessary facilities. Berg en Dal already has a mother-and-child house, a large Pluryn location, and wants to realize skaeve huse.

110042.explanation: Recreation and tourism are an important economic pillar.

110043.explanation: This should be possible in certain places.

110044.explanation: Only if they are able to do so.

110045.explanation: We must support residents with little money.

110048.explanation: We are already doing this well.

110051.explanation: Price and quality are both important.

110052.explanation: That is not high with us.

110054.explanation: It has not been increased for 5 years, so it must be adjusted now. It is reasonable that tourists also contribute to our high level of facilities.

110057.explanation: We are already doing that and will continue to do so; otherwise, the asylum chain will become even more congested.

110058.explanation: With us, a lot of waste can already be disposed of for free.

110059.explanation: Sustainability is important. However, we strive for local ownership.

110061.explanation: We should not place too many tasks on primary schools.

110064.explanation: What is high?? Tall flats like in cities do not fit in our environment. But in some places, a building with 6-8 floors is suitable. It requires a tailored approach.

110069.explanation: Museums are important for recreation and tourism and for our identity.

110014.explanation: It is about the quality of the alderman as a person. The members of the executive board must possess sufficient knowledge and skills for the office. LOKAAL! also has a balanced male/female distribution in the board.

110015.explanation: We choose real resident participation via village agendas, youth municipal councils, and early involvement in projects instead of referenda as a standard instrument.

110018.explanation: Side activities are possible if they are small-scale, fit within nature and landscape, and conform to the established policy for the rural area.

110019.explanation: No direct operational subsidies; however, support is provided through advice, process guidance, and involvement of residents in solar projects, in line with the solar ladder (first roofs/facades/infrastructure).

110021.explanation: No preference for an asylum seekers' center in the municipality of Berg en Dal; we take our responsibility by providing shelter for unaccompanied minor asylum seekers according to the distribution law.

110022.explanation: Employers are responsible for housing, but this does not necessarily have to be on their own grounds. Housing on holiday parks contradicts purely recreational use and is not allowed by us.

110023.explanation: For every tree cut down, we want to plant at least two new trees for greening and cooling (climate adaptation).

110030.explanation: We do this through support via the Doemeeregeling and indirectly through subsidies to sports clubs. Children growing up in families with little financial means are additionally supported by the Leergeld foundation with, for example, school supplies, a bike, laptop, and/or membership fees.

110035.explanation: Monuments say something about our identity and are our cultural memory. Well-maintained monuments make the village attractive for residents and also for tourists.

110036.explanation: We have recently invested in extra BOA capacity for enforcement on safety, littering, and dog waste.

110037.explanation: Safety is important, but privacy is too. Temporary camera surveillance can only be considered at nuisance locations. It must be proportional and careful. For example, at illegal dumping sites of underground containers!

110038.explanation: We organize protection where it is necessary. We do this together with municipalities in the region. It is not necessary to have a domestic violence shelter in every village. The municipality of Berg en Dal already has many care facilities.

110042.explanation: Holiday homes are for recreation; permanent residence undermines the tourist profile and the livability of holiday parks.

110043.explanation: The need for housing is unprecedentedly high. Adding a small street or neighborhood will help enormously and benefit livability in the centers. The possibilities differ greatly per village center. An extra neighborhood must fit well into the landscape. We also look for possibilities within the village center.

110044.explanation: Socially meaningful work where appropriate; with leniency for those who demonstrably cannot do so.

110045.explanation: Waivers are an important tool for residents with a low income; poverty is not a choice.

110048.explanation: The number of charging points grows along with the number of electric cars. This is part of our broader sustainability approach.

110049.explanation: We lower the speed to 30 km/h where safety and livability require it. We take into account the frameworks set by the province. Elsewhere, we carefully consider traffic flow.

110050.explanation: The quality and availability of care are paramount. Choice based on faith or culture is not always feasible within the available capacity.

110051.explanation: We select based on quality and effectiveness; procurement and contracting are largely regional and not solely based on price.

110052.explanation: In the municipality of Berg en Dal, the property tax is already below the national average. A realistic rate is necessary to maintain facilities, especially given the uncertainty of the municipal fund after 2027.

110054.explanation: An appropriate rate fits our tourist facilities and costs; a rate increase has already been planned for 2027. The municipality of Berg en Dal has a lot to offer tourists.

110057.explanation: According to regional allocations, status holders are given priority. At the same time, we guard the balance with local allocation for our own residents and starters. We want to increase the allocation of existing housing stock to residents of Berg en Dal to at least 50%.

110058.explanation: Many waste streams are already free; for some waste streams a small contribution applies from the principle 'the polluter pays'. We reduce litter through education, neighborhood heroes, extra bins, and enforcement.

110059.explanation: We follow the principle of the so-called 'solar ladder'. This means we first opt for solar panels on roofs, facades, and infrastructure. Not on agricultural land. This also protects the open character of our polder landscape.

110061.explanation: Schools determine their own educational content. The municipality facilitates but does not prescribe educational programs.

110064.explanation: We allow low-rise apartments where spatially fitting. High residential towers do not fit in the green rural environment of Groesbeek.

110066.explanation: For new construction, we follow parking standards; in existing villages we opt for greening and less paving.

110068.explanation: We will continue to strive for less waste and better separation. Especially the weekly summer collection of organic waste deserves extra attention.

110069.explanation: We invest where necessary for preservation, but museums should be as self-supporting as possible. We take a programmatic look at the Freedom Museum and the Museumpark Oriëntalis. The Africa Museum remains uncertain.

110014.explanation: Equality is an important principle for GroenLinks-PvdA. A college with as many men as women better represents society. This applies more broadly: everyone must have equal opportunities to participate and have influence. Therefore, we consciously aim for fair and diverse representation.

110015.explanation: Residents must be able to think along and discuss important choices. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to use powerful democratic instruments, such as a referendum and especially a citizens’ assembly, to give residents real influence. We take village agendas seriously as an important basis for local policy.

110018.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports these types of activities at farms. We want the municipality to support farmers towards a sustainable future. Such activities stimulate local and sustainable food, small-scale tourism, and direct contact between producer and consumer in our municipality.

110019.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality to support such initiatives. Energy must be clean, local, fair, and affordable for everyone. Thanks to our efforts, Berg en Dal is working on a Municipal Energy Company for sustainable, local, affordable energy.

110021.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that refugees should preferably be accommodated in small-scale centers spread across the entire municipality. So far, reception in our municipality runs well and causes no problems. Information evenings and good cooperation with local residents are very important for this.

110022.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture can, according to GroenLinks-PvdA, house seasonal workers on their own company premises, up to 20 people. Social cohesion in the neighborhood is important. Vulnerable workers must be protected against exploitation and poor housing.

110023.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants two new trees planted for every tree cut down. This way we do not just compensate; we also strengthen biodiversity and greenery in our municipality. Moreover, small trees must first mature.

110030.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes sport is important for everyone. We want to apply the current 'Doe Mee' (Join In) scheme of 100 euros more generously and actively support residents and/or clubs, so that people with a lower disposable income can more easily become members and participate.

110035.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes the preservation of monuments is important. Maintenance and restoration are partly funded by the municipality and province, supplemented by funds and fundraising, so these valuable buildings remain for future generations.

110036.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds safety important, but the deployment of municipal enforcement officers must be smart and effective. Extra officers can help, especially at places and times where truly needed, and in combination with community workers, prevention, and cooperation with residents and partners.

110037.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA values safety. This involves optimal use of prevention. Camera surveillance also costs money and must be used carefully with respect for privacy. Nevertheless, cameras can help in specific locations, only where really necessary.

110038.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes victims of domestic violence deserve a safe place. A women’s shelter in Berg en Dal can contribute to this, with good support and cooperation with regional shelters, so that women, men, non-binary individuals, and children get the help they need.

110042.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday homes obstructs tourism, while the locations are not built for this. More small and simple homes are welcome; creative use of holiday homes in winter can offer solutions, but not through permanent residence.

110043.explanation: New construction outside existing village boundaries can impair the landscape and livability. The municipality should therefore concentrate primarily within village centers. Only in exceptional cases and with well-integrated plans can such solutions be allowed.

110044.explanation: Many people want to participate and contribute to society if given opportunities. GroenLinks-PvdA believes the municipality can stimulate this by active policies, but compulsory community work for social assistance recipients is not the right approach.

110045.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes for residents with a low disposable income supports the most vulnerable, so everyone can participate and financial pressure is limited. In the long term, this yields more than it costs because stable residents actively contribute to society.

110048.explanation: To stimulate the growth of electric cars and improve air quality, a sufficient supply of charging points is needed, now and in the future.

110049.explanation: In all village centers, we want to reduce the maximum speed to 30 km/h to create safe and livable streets for residents and cyclists everywhere. For new building projects, GroenLinks-PvdA prefers home zones where traffic moves at walking pace and children can safely play on the street.

110050.explanation: It is important that people can choose a healthcare provider that fits their faith, culture or belief. This way, care feels appropriate and familiar and works better in the long term than when the municipality or institutions assign this from above.

110051.explanation: In youth care, quality must come first, especially in severe cases. Often the provider with the lowest price is chosen. GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality, when selecting youth care organizations, to focus primarily on the best care that works best.

110052.explanation: The property tax (OZB) takes into account the residents' ability to pay and is used efficiently. Lowering it would increase other taxes; therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA opposes reducing the property tax.

110054.explanation: Tourists use facilities that the municipality contributes to, such as ferries, benches, walking and cycling routes, and natural areas. GroenLinks-PvdA believes the tourist tax does not need to be increased beyond what was planned. Revenue should primarily be invested in maintaining these facilities.

110057.explanation: Status holders deserve a safe place to live. In Berg en Dal, they currently make up 6% of the social housing stock. Not providing them with housing immediately, forcing them to stay longer in a reception center or end up on the streets, is inhumane, undesirable, and can actually cause problems.

110058.explanation: It is important that everyone can dispose of their waste safely and responsibly. Free disposal of special waste at the recycling center makes this accessible to everyone and encourages reuse and recycling, contributing to a cleaner and more sustainable municipality.

110059.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary and preferably generated as locally as possible. In Berg en Dal, there are not enough roofs available, so agricultural land must also be used. An ideal mix includes solar panels, wind turbines, and hydropower. We combine energy generation with nature, biodiversity, and agriculture, respecting the landscape.

110061.explanation: Local culture and language are valuable and provide identity and connection. Attention to the Kleverlands dialect in primary schools can contribute to this. The municipality can enable and support this, even though in practice it can be challenging due to lack of teachers and materials.

110064.explanation: To build affordably and offer housing opportunities to our youth, it is sometimes necessary to build upwards. We do not need residential towers. However, building too low leads to high prices, as is currently seen with two- or three-room apartments costing several hundred thousand euros.

110066.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces in (shopping) centers. In residential neighborhoods, GroenLinks-PvdA prefers green spaces and playgrounds over additional parking, with a preference for home zones. We encourage cycling and car sharing, and consider people who work at times when public transport is not yet running.

110068.explanation: More frequent household waste collection raises waste charges, which is undesirable. Moreover, less frequent collection promotes waste separation and recycling. GroenLinks-PvdA believes costs, sustainability, and convenience should be weighed when collecting household waste.

110069.explanation: Museums play an important role in culture, education, community spirit, and tourism. We want to develop a cultural policy that provides museums and other cultural organizations with clarity about support, so they can plan their work properly and remain accessible to everyone.

110014.explanation: An equal distribution is nice, but the quality of the aldermen is the primary concern. In the past three terms, this has not caused any problems in our municipality.

110015.explanation: The CDA believes that residents are represented through the municipal council and that there are already many opportunities for participation.

110018.explanation: Most agricultural businesses already do this, such as Hazelaarshof in Kekerdom or the winemakers in our municipality. It is good for the vitality of farms and the livability of the countryside.

110019.explanation: The municipality should facilitate these kinds of initiatives, not directly finance them.

110021.explanation: Our municipality already does a lot relative to many other municipalities, such as housing Ukrainians and unaccompanied minor asylum seekers (AMVs).

110022.explanation: Examine possibilities per location, also considering environmental effects.

110023.explanation: The CDA stands for a green living environment.

110030.explanation: There are already many arrangements in our municipality for people with low incomes.

110035.explanation: The municipality must take care of the monuments within its borders.

110036.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) have limited authority. The CDA believes the police should be more visible. Residents should also address each other more regarding undesirable behavior.

110037.explanation: The CDA supports more camera surveillance in known unsafe areas because of its preventive effect.

110038.explanation: A regional facility for this already exists in Nijmegen.

110042.explanation: There is a lot of improper use of holiday homes. The municipality should offer alternatives in addition to enforcement.

110043.explanation: Given the housing challenge, infill alone is not sufficient. Adding ‘a street’ per village is desirable but must be carefully integrated into the landscape.

110044.explanation: This already happens in our municipality and there are good experiences with it. It should be further expanded.

110045.explanation: For a very small group of residents with very low income, this arrangement exists. The CDA believes it should be retained.

110048.explanation: The municipality already has a comprehensive plan for charging points, but sufficient regular parking spaces must remain available.

110049.explanation: Assess per situation whether 30 km/h is feasible, considering traffic flow, emergency response times, and ownership (provincial roads).

110050.explanation: This is undesirable. Moreover, it is a luxury that society cannot afford.

110051.explanation: According to the CDA, quality should be leading.

110052.explanation: The CDA supports low local taxes. As far as the municipality's financial position allows, the property tax (ozb) can be reduced. Social facilities such as community centers should be compensated for the property tax.

110054.explanation: As a tourist municipality, we do not want to increase the tourist tax further. We should not price ourselves out of the market.

110057.explanation: The municipality must house status holders in accordance with national policy.

110058.explanation: Most types of waste can already be disposed of for free at the recycling center.

110059.explanation: Available farmland is needed for food supply. Moreover, we want to handle the landscape carefully.

110061.explanation: We appreciate the preservation of the dialect but see no role for the municipality in this.

110064.explanation: In the villages, building somewhat higher is allowed to meet housing needs and to keep the surrounding countryside open.

110066.explanation: As a rurally located municipality, we must retain sufficient parking spaces. In practice, we see many parking spots disappearing.

110068.explanation: During the summer months, we particularly want organic waste (GFT) to be collected more often.

110069.explanation: Museums receive funds from a new scheme. According to the CDA, this is sufficient.

110014.explanation: We are in favor of a balanced and diverse municipal executive, but since it involves a small number of people, having an equal number of men and women is not a strict requirement.

110015.explanation: This could be a good idea for important topics. However, organizing it is expensive and referenda often lack nuance. We believe it is more important that residents are well and timely informed about developments in their environment and can immediately contribute their thoughts.

110018.explanation: That depends, among other things, on the location of the business and the quality of the plan for a secondary activity, such as a shop or campsite. But in principle, we are not opposed to it if it helps farmers keep their businesses profitable.

110019.explanation: Of course, only if these are good plans that are genuinely sustainable and fit within municipal policy. So no 'free money'...

110021.explanation: We want Berg en Dal to contribute to the reception of refugees, but spread across the villages. So not one (relatively) large asylum seekers center, but multiple local reception locations for small groups.

110022.explanation: This can be a good way to house seasonal workers. There are good and bad examples. The most important thing is that the surroundings are not disturbed and that seasonal workers are not exploited.

110023.explanation: This is already municipal policy. Because older trees are more valuable, we actually believe that more new young trees should be planted when an old tree is cut down. The older the tree, the more new ones should be planted.

110030.explanation: We find sports and exercise very important. The municipality of Berg en Dal offers residents who earn little but still want to exercise a fixed reimbursement of €100. We consider this a good scheme.

110035.explanation: The monumental buildings in our municipality make the municipality more beautiful and remind us of the history of our villages. We consider this important, so it may cost something.

110036.explanation: The mayor has explained that this is not necessary now. The number of enforcement officers (boa's) we have is sufficient.

110037.explanation: Camera footage can be useful in solving crimes, but camera surveillance also affects people's privacy. The number of crimes in our municipality is low, so extra camera surveillance is not necessary. If that changes, we can discuss it.

110038.explanation: These kinds of shelters are important, but in a small municipality like Berg en Dal, they are expensive and ineffective. We can better support a regional women's shelter.

110042.explanation: Holiday homes are not full-fledged residences and different rules apply. In addition, they are important for tourism in our municipality. But if owners of holiday parks want to build full-fledged homes on their park (or a part of it), we are definitely open to that!

110043.explanation: We want to protect the countryside, nature, and the appearance of our villages. That’s why we want to realize as many homes as possible within the existing village boundaries. But exceptions are possible.

110044.explanation: The reasons why people receive social assistance benefits vary greatly. For many people, part-time work or volunteer work is good, but the fact remains that not everyone can work. Therefore, we do not want to make this mandatory.

110045.explanation: It is assessed on a case-by-case basis whether this is necessary. If we stop doing this, some people will get into big trouble. We must prevent that.

110048.explanation: The expectation is that the number of electric cars will increase rapidly in the Netherlands. The number of charging points is currently not growing fast enough to keep up, and it is difficult to catch up later. Therefore, we want to designate more charging points now.

110049.explanation: Traffic is getting busier, also within built-up areas. This leads to more dangerous situations. To prevent accidents, we want to lower the maximum speed. We also need to carefully consider ways to route traffic around built-up areas.

110050.explanation: Healthcare in the Netherlands is under pressure. Distinction based on religion, culture, or beliefs only makes it more difficult. Every healthcare provider must be able to help everyone who needs care. Always with respect for each other.

110051.explanation: Price is an important factor, but it is about price-quality ratio: what do you get in return? We have no use for a cheap organization that provides poor youth care.

110052.explanation: The financial situation of municipalities will deteriorate in the coming years. A limited increase of the property tax (ozb) is one way to address this. The ozb in Berg en Dal is currently quite low. We find it justifiable to increase it slightly, but not higher than in comparable municipalities.

110054.explanation: Tourists also use the facilities in our municipality. Therefore, it is reasonable that they pay taxes. However, we believe that part of the increase should be specifically spent on facilities that enhance the tourist quality of Berg en Dal.

110057.explanation: This is already the case. The municipality prioritizes people with a worse position in the rental market, such as status holders, but also caregivers and people staying in women's shelters. Less than 10% of social rental homes in our municipality go to status holders.

110058.explanation: We support the principle 'the polluter pays'. If we made this free, municipal taxes would have to rise (further). We do not want that.

110059.explanation: D66 prefers other solutions for the energy transition (wind turbines, hydropower, district heating, etc.). Solar panels on agricultural land are a much less good option but may still be necessary to become CO2-neutral.

110061.explanation: The Kleverlands dialect is part of the culture and history of the villages in our municipality. We support the municipality helping schools to pay attention to this.

110064.explanation: We find buildings with several floors, like now at the Market Square, acceptable. But tall apartment blocks do not fit in our villages.

110066.explanation: We must try to use the car less and walk, cycle, or use public transport more. We want to encourage this with better cycle paths, parking facilities, etc., but also by reducing the number of car parking spaces, especially in new housing projects.

110068.explanation: We believe that waste collection now happens often enough. For example, in the warmer months, organic waste (GFT) is collected more frequently. Collecting more often is very expensive.

110069.explanation: We find museums and other cultural facilities in our municipality very important. Therefore, we want a good plan for all those facilities. So not just throwing more money at one or two museums.

110014.explanation: Not mandatory; quality is the most important criterion.

110015.explanation: The Berg en Dal VVD supports our ordinary democracy with elected representatives, and citizen participation should take shape in other ways.

110018.explanation: More vitality in the countryside also means a different revenue model for farmers. This helps ensure the succession and survival of farms and the maintenance of our landscape.

110019.explanation: Initiators should not automatically receive a subsidy. As Berg en Dal VVD, we assess that per project.

110021.explanation: Our municipality has now contributed for several years, and many municipalities around us still do not meet the required numbers. In our view, it is time for those municipalities to take responsibility.

110022.explanation: This can limit nuisance. But good housing is always better than a caravan on your own property.

110023.explanation: Some trees are old and worn out (rotten) and need to be cut down. New ones do not have to be planted for these.

110030.explanation: We do partly pay to ensure, especially children, continue to participate in sports. This is a preventive measure for healthcare costs.

110035.explanation: In the end, we can only spend our money once. Cutting back on monument care can be done by designating fewer municipal monuments and relaxing the rules.

110036.explanation: Three enforcement officers (boa's) and one supervisor are far too few for the entire municipality. As Berg en Dal VVD, we have supported more for years.

110037.explanation: Cameras create a false sense of security and are useless if immediate action cannot be taken.

110038.explanation: In the neighboring municipality of Nijmegen, there is already a shelter where victims can go.

110042.explanation: In certain areas of our municipality, this can be arranged well, such as at Cantecleer.

110043.explanation: We simply do not have the luxury to oppose this. Every housing development site is important in the fight against the housing shortage.

110044.explanation: When possible and desired, this can help prevent someone from becoming isolated. As Berg en Dal VVD, we are very much in favor of this.

110045.explanation: The municipality is not here to pursue income politics or to level incomes. That is the responsibility of the national government.

110048.explanation: Berg en Dal VVD also wants to contribute to emission-free driving by offering sufficient charging spots. Not only for the residents of our municipality, but also for the many tourists who visit each year. They too don't want to be confronted with empty batteries.

110049.explanation: We only consider this a good idea in residential neighborhoods. On major through roads, this could possibly be applied in certain spots with a 30 km/h limit, but that requires a tailored approach.

110050.explanation: Healthcare costs are already very high in the Netherlands and the more restrictions there are, the higher these costs become. As far as we're concerned, there is already a lot of freedom of choice in healthcare in the Netherlands, and it does not need to be expanded further.

110051.explanation: Especially on price and also on quality. Youth care is an important part of our municipality and also accounts for a substantial part of our budget. That is why paying attention to price is essential.

110052.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is a housing tax used to cover the municipality's financial shortfalls, while the municipality does not have to perform many tasks. We should cut costs there first before looking at income measures.

110054.explanation: We do not consider it wise to increase the tourist tax at a time when many Dutch people are struggling financially. These people especially need a holiday, and Berg en Dal municipality should welcome them rather than try to drive them away.

110057.explanation: As Berg en Dal VVD, we want to remove this target group from the housing ordinance. It is unfair for people who have been on a waiting list for years to be passed over by people who have just arrived in the Netherlands.

110058.explanation: This is already free and should remain so.

110059.explanation: Berg en Dal VVD clearly chooses the future with the construction of small nuclear power plants. These can easily meet our energy demand. That is why we do not want additional solar fields on land nor wind turbines installed.

110061.explanation: The local dialect in our municipality is very important. For example, carnival is celebrated in grand style in all our villages, with dialect. The dialect is fading somewhat into the background, but through this measure we can be proud of our dialect again.

110064.explanation: For Berg en Dal VVD, 'high' means a maximum of 5 or 6 stories. Real skyscrapers belong in the city.

110066.explanation: At the edges of village centers this is desirable, but within the village centers it may be desirable to discourage car use. Adding parking spaces at the edges of these centers can achieve that.

110068.explanation: In the summer months this is currently substandard due to a shortage of drivers. Meanwhile, costs are rising and we get less quality. That is a situation turned upside down.

110069.explanation: As Berg en Dal VVD, we advocate a rational, economic approach to museums. They should preferably be supported if they have a clear role in social participation, tourism, or economic value, and less so if they rely solely on subsidies without own revenue potential.

statements.title: The municipality should put more money into museums.
There should be more camera surveillance in Berg en Dal.

titleParts.text: In Berg en Dal moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The residents of Berg en Dal should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Berg en Dal moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Berg en Dal should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.text: In Berg en Dal moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
High-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Groesbeek.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
A women's shelter should be established in Berg en Dal.

titleParts.text: In Berg en Dal moet een

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include the Kleverlands dialect in their curriculum.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the villages.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Waste should be collected from homes more frequently.
